_note: ORIGINAL content. Topic coverage was checked against Think C++ and the Google C++ Class outline. No prose, examples, or exercises were copied; the references are curriculum guides only.
title: Exceptions
summary: Report a failure without pretending the program can keep going normally.
refresher: A C++ exception reports a problem across function calls. `throw` sends the problem out. `try` marks code that may throw. `catch` handles a specific kind of problem. Catch only what you can handle.

blocks.body: An exception means the code hit a problem it cannot handle in the normal path. Instead of returning a fake answer, it reports the failure to a place that may know what to do.
`throw` sends the problem out. `try` surrounds the risky work. `catch` handles a matching problem. Keep the try block small so the failing line is easier to find.

blocks.title: The shape is similar to Python
blocks.body: Python uses `try`, `raise`, and `except`. C++ uses `try`, `throw`, and `catch`. In both languages, expected choices still belong in ordinary `if` statements.

blocks.caption: The function either returns a positive value or throws a message. Main catches that specific failure and prints the explanation.
A catch-all that does nothing hides the cause. Catch a meaningful type where a useful response is possible.

blocks.body: For the negative input, `requirePositive` cannot fulfill its rule, so it throws `invalid_argument`. The normal path stops. Main catches that type, reads its message, and then continues after the catch block.

blocks.title: Exceptions are not ordinary branch logic
blocks.body: Use an `if` for an expected choice, such as an empty search result. Use an exception when a function cannot complete the job it promised and a caller needs the failure information.

blocks.prompt: How should standard exceptions usually be caught?
blocks.why: Catching by const reference avoids copying and preserves the dynamic exception information for the handler.

blocks.prompt: What happens to local objects when an exception leaves their block?
choices: They are cleaned up as the block is exited
blocks.why: C++ exits each active block on the way to a matching catch, so fully created local objects run their normal cleanup.

blocks.title: Exceptions checklist
items: Throw when the normal answer would be dishonest.
Catch the specific problem you can handle.
Do not hide errors you still need to fix.
